2026-04-15T23:21:00.1018671Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-15T23:21:00.1019228Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-15T23:21:00.1019714Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-15T23:21:00.1020091Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-15T23:21:00.1020406Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-15T23:21:00.1020639Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-15T23:24:05.8842823Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-15T23:22:08.820486505Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58192,\"title\":\"[Communication Messages] Docs: Add Unified Messaging client documentation to README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.Communication.Messages`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/`\\n**Triggered by:** [REDACTED] (PR #56911) by `@MaiLinhP`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #56911 migrated `Azure.Communication.Messages` to the new TypeSpec emitter. As part of this migration, two new public client classes (`ConversationAdministrationClient` and `ConversationThreadClient`) are now present in the package as part of the Unified Messaging feature (introduced in v1.3.0-beta.1). Additionally, message types added in v1.1.0 through v1.3.0-beta.2 (document/video/audio notification content, interactive messages, reaction messages, sticker messages, read receipts, typing indicators) remain undocumented in the README.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `ConversationAdministrationClient` is a public class with no documentation in README — not mentioned in Key concepts, no usage examples, and no authentication guidance\\n- `ConversationThreadClient` is a public class (authenticated via `CommunicationTokenCredential`) with no documentation in README — not mentioned in Key concepts and no usage examples\\n- \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section only lists `NotificationMessagesClient` and is missing the two Conversation clients\\n- `DocumentNotificationContent`, `VideoNotificationContent`, and `AudioNotificationContent` added in v1.1.0 have no examples (only `ImageNotificationContent` is shown)\\n- Interactive Message, Reaction Message, and Sticker Message types added in v1.2.0-beta.1 are not documented\\n- Read receipt and typing indicator (v1.3.0-beta.2) are not documented\\n- \\u0026#34;Using statements\\u0026#34; and \\u0026#34;Authenticate the client\\u0026#34; subsections are placed under \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; instead of under \\u0026#34;Getting started\\u0026#34; as required by the standard README template\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\n1. Move the \\u0026#34;Using statements\\u0026#34; and \\u0026#34;Authenticate the client\\u0026#34; content from \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; into \\u0026#34;Getting started\\u0026#34;, after the \\u0026#34;Prerequisites\\u0026#34; subsection.\\n\\n2. Expand the \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section to describe all four main clients:\\n   - `NotificationMessagesClient` — sends one-way notification messages (WhatsApp / SMS)\\n   - `MessageTemplateClient` — retrieves message templates\\n   - `ConversationAdministrationClient` — creates and manages conversation threads (service-side, uses connection string or `AzureKeyCredential`)\\n   - `ConversationThreadClient` — sends and receives messages within a conversation thread (participant-side, uses `CommunicationTokenCredential`)\\n\\n3. Add an \\u0026#34;Authenticate the client\\u0026#34; subsection for the Conversation clients. Example:\\n\\n```\\nvar connectionString = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\nConversationAdministrationClient adminClient = new ConversationAdministrationClient(connectionString);\\n```\\n\\n````\\nvar endpoint = new Uri(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\nvar communicationTokenCredential = new CommunicationTokenCredential(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\nConversationThreadClient threadClient = new ConversationThreadClient(endpoint, communicationTokenCredential);\\n```\\n\\n4. Add an \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; subsection for Unified Messaging (conversation operations), e.g., listing conversations and sending a conversation message.\\n\\n5. Add examples for the media types missing from README: `DocumentNotificationContent`, `VideoNotificationContent`, `AudioNotificationContent`.\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nThe repository uses `Update-Snippets.ps1` to inject code from test files into README placeholders. Snippets should be written in a test file and referenced in the README.\\n\\n**Snippet test file path:** `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/tests/Samples/Sample_ConversationClients.cs`\\n\\nCreate the test file with sample methods for:\\n- Creating a `ConversationAdministrationClient` (snippet: `Azure_Communication_Messages_ConversationAdministrationClientCreate`)\\n- Creating a `ConversationThreadClient` (snippet: `Azure_Communication_Messages_ConversationThreadClientCreate`)\\n\\nWrap each block with `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion`. 2026-04-15T23:24:05.8900639Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-15T23:22:09.078874421Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58192,\"title\":\"[Communication Messages] Docs: Add Unified Messaging client documentation to README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.Communication.Messages`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/`\\n**Triggered by:** [REDACTED] (PR #56911) by `@MaiLinhP`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #56911 migrated `Azure.Communication.Messages` to the new TypeSpec emitter. As part of this migration, two new public client classes (`ConversationAdministrationClient` and `ConversationThreadClient`) are now present in the package as part of the Unified Messaging feature (introduced in v1.3.0-beta.1). Additionally, message types added in v1.1.0 through v1.3.0-beta.2 (document/video/audio notification content, interactive messages, reaction messages, sticker messages, read receipts, typing indicators) remain undocumented in the README.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `ConversationAdministrationClient` is a public class with no documentation in README — not mentioned in Key concepts, no usage examples, and no authentication guidance\\n- `ConversationThreadClient` is a public class (authenticated via `CommunicationTokenCredential`) with no documentation in README — not mentioned in Key concepts and no usage examples\\n- \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section only lists `NotificationMessagesClient` and is missing the two Conversation clients\\n- `DocumentNotificationContent`, `VideoNotificationContent`, and `AudioNotificationContent` added in v1.1.0 have no examples (only `ImageNotificationContent` is shown)\\n- Interactive Message, Reaction Message, and Sticker Message types added in v1.2.0-beta.1 are not documented\\n- Read receipt and typing indicator (v1.3.0-beta.2) are not documented\\n- \\u0026#34;Using statements\\u0026#34; and \\u0026#34;Authenticate the client\\u0026#34; subsections are placed under \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; instead of under \\u0026#34;Getting started\\u0026#34; as required by the standard README template\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\n1. Move the \\u0026#34;Using statements\\u0026#34; and \\u0026#34;Authenticate the client\\u0026#34; content from \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; into \\u0026#34;Getting started\\u0026#34;, after the \\u0026#34;Prerequisites\\u0026#34; subsection.\\n\\n2. Expand the \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section to describe all four main clients:\\n   - `NotificationMessagesClient` — sends one-way notification messages (WhatsApp / SMS)\\n   - `MessageTemplateClient` — retrieves message templates\\n   - `ConversationAdministrationClient` — creates and manages conversation threads (service-side, uses connection string or `AzureKeyCredential`)\\n   - `ConversationThreadClient` — sends and receives messages within a conversation thread (participant-side, uses `CommunicationTokenCredential`)\\n\\n3. Add an \\u0026#34;Authenticate the client\\u0026#34; subsection for the Conversation clients. Example:\\n\\n```\\nvar connectionString = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\nConversationAdministrationClient adminClient = new ConversationAdministrationClient(connectionString);\\n```\\n\\n````\\nvar endpoint = new Uri(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\nvar communicationTokenCredential = new CommunicationTokenCredential(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\nConversationThreadClient threadClient = new ConversationThreadClient(endpoint, communicationTokenCredential);\\n```\\n\\n4. Add an \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; subsection for Unified Messaging (conversation operations), e.g., listing conversations and sending a conversation message.\\n\\n5. Add examples for the media types missing from README: `DocumentNotificationContent`, `VideoNotificationContent`, `AudioNotificationContent`.\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nThe repository uses `Update-Snippets.ps1` to inject code from test files into README placeholders. Snippets should be written in a test file and referenced in the README.\\n\\n**Snippet test file path:** `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/tests/Samples/Sample_ConversationClients.cs`\\n\\nCreate the test file with sample methods for:\\n- Creating a `ConversationAdministrationClient` (snippet: `Azure_Communication_Messages_ConversationAdministrationClientCreate`)\\n- Creating a `ConversationThreadClient` (snippet: `Azure_Communication_Messages_ConversationThreadClientCreate`)\\n\\nWrap each block with `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion`.
